_purpose: Orchestrator continuity across Claude Code sessions. The HUMAN talks to ONE AI (the orchestrator); that AI dispatches scope-specific work via the Agent tool. When the human starts a fresh Claude Code session, the new orchestrator MUST read this file early in startup so it knows: what was last being worked on, what's pending, what decisions were just made, what scopes are currently claimed, what to NOT re-do.
_protocol: Update this file at every significant decision/checkpoint. Treat it like a running session journal — newest at the bottom of recent_decisions, oldest pruned. The aggregator script `node scripts/agent-status.js` complements this with per-scope status from data/coordination/sessions/*.json.
_what_vs_why: As of v0.283.x: this file records WHY decisions were made (running journal). `docs/PRIORITIES.md` records WHAT comes next (strict-ranked backlog). Both must be updated when scope changes. Don't put 'next action' here — put it in PRIORITIES.md.
current_orchestrator_session: post-cutover hardening sweep CLOSED (2026-05-19) — all 6 security tasks shipped; Inc6 attended wipe is the only remaining gate

recent_decisions.decision: v0.262.4 — trimmed obsolete Server-policy UI (operator: trimmed of obsolete options now, after confirming relay recovered + R4 Roles table correct on live DB: Unverified 280/5/4 ... Admin 10000/500/500 = correct base_tier backfill). Removed the now-empty Per-role limits subsection header + its dead pointer hint (no controls under it post-R4) + the stale matrix code comment. Rewrote the Server policy intro hint to accurately describe what REMAINS (server-wide only: sharing/voice/streaming masters, require-PQ, total disk cap, allowed extensions). Kept everything genuinely server-wide. Pure UI text/structure removal — NO migration, NO relay logic, zero upgrade-path risk (deliberately verified that, given the prior incident). cargo native + 3 lints green.
recent_decisions.rationale: Post-R4 the Per-role-limits block was a header with no controls + a breadcrumb — clutter. Intro hint was factually wrong (claimed per-role limits live here). Low-risk UI-only cleanup; explicitly noted no migration so it cannot repeat the v0.262.0 ordering incident.

recent_decisions.decision: PRODUCTION INCIDENT + HOTFIX v0.262.2. Relay crash-looped (nginx 502, total outage; Deploy-to-VPS exit 3). Ground truth via ssh humanity-vps journalctl: panic src/relay/mod.rs:141 "table roles has no column named can_image_share". ROOT CAUSE: v0.261 (worsened v0.262) added new columns to BOTH the roles CREATE TABLE and the seed INSERT, but the seed ran BEFORE the idempotent ALTERs. Fresh DBs: CREATE makes the cols → every fresh-DB test passed → bug shipped. Live relay.db: CREATE IF NOT EXISTS no-op (old 10-col table) → seed INSERT references missing column → Storage::open expect() panic → systemd crash-loop. The upgrade path was NEVER tested. FIX: moved both guarded ALTER blocks (v0.261 image/file + v0.262 R4 + backfill) to run BEFORE the seed loop; seed is now INSERT OR IGNORE post-ALTER (correct on fresh AND upgrade). Added regression test upgrade_from_pre_v0261_roles_schema_does_not_panic (rewinds roles to pre-v0.261 shape, reopens Storage — panics pre-fix, clean post-fix). 6 roles tests + 3 lints + relay build green. LESSON: any migration that adds columns to a CREATE TABLE must add them via idempotent ALTER too, and the ALTERs must precede any seed/INSERT that names them; ALWAYS add an upgrade-path test (rewind schema + reopen) — fresh-DB tests cannot catch ordering bugs.

v0.262 — R4 SHIPPED (operator chose Full R4). RoleDef now owns max_chars/max_upload_mb/max_uploads_kept (serde-default = conservative unverified numbers; appended positional SQL idx 12/13/14, params ?13/14/15). roles table +3 cols; fresh seed = canonical per-tier numbers (unverified 280/5/4 ... admin 10000/500/500); idempotent ALTER + NON-BREAKING backfill UPDATE that sets each existing role from the LIVE server_settings value of its old base_tier (correlated CASE subquery) → effective numbers identical post-upgrade. Enforcement rewired to role_def(role).max_* (relay.rs chat len; api.rs upload FIFO + size) — dropped the base_tier→server_settings hop; ALSO closed a latent gap: per-tier max_upload_mb was never enforced server-side (only a hard const) — now min(hard ceiling, role.max_upload_mb). UI: per-role-limits matrix REMOVED; Roles grid is the ONE cohesive table (Role|Color|Stream|Upload|Voice|Image|File|MaxChars|MaxUpMB|UpKept|Save|Delete, 12 cols, built-ins numbers editable too); add-form base_tier combo → Prefill… preset (copies canonical numbers, sets legacy base_tier). server_settings.max_*_{tier} kept as inert back-compat shadows. 5 roles tests (sharing roundtrip, numeric roundtrip, canonical-seed, default-deny, sharing-on) + native+relay + 3 lints green. roles-system.md R4 marked BUILT.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator asked 3x for the unified per-role section + chose Full R4 explicitly. Non-breaking backfill from live server_settings is mandatory for the live VPS. Enforcing the previously-decorative per-tier max_upload_mb is the no-bandaid bonus. base_tier kept as inert prefill/migration-source (not deleted) to avoid a pointless destructive schema drop.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.261.0/.1 per-role image/file sharing SHIPPED to GitHub origin (deploy source) + gh releases created. FORGE MIRROR BEHIND: git.united-humanity.us:22 timed out (host unreachable, NOT auth) — per CLAUDE.md did NOT switch to HTTPS, did NOT loop-retry; forge is a mirror, origin drives deploy. Forge is missing v0.261.0/.1 (main + tags); resync when host reachable: git push forge main && git push forge v0.261.0 v0.261.1. Lesson: chained pushes with 2>&1|tail -1 MASK git exit codes (the && chain continued past a failed forge push and a skipped gh release) — verify pushes with explicit git ls-remote / gh release view, not piped tails.
recent_decisions.rationale: Origin + releases are the contract (deploy + version SOP); forge is best-effort mirror. Recorded the tail-mask footgun so future ships verify origin/release explicitly.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.261 — PQ-roles Phase 1: per-role image/file sharing (operator chose Both). RoleDef +can_image_share/+can_file_share (#[serde(default)] false; Default-impl false = default-deny for unknown roles). roles table: CREATE +2 cols DEFAULT 1, seed all 5 built-ins to 1, idempotent ALTER DEFAULT 1 for existing DBs → upgrade NON-BREAKING (sharing stays gated only by server master exactly as before; per-role denial is additive/opt-in). list_roles/role_def SELECT appended cols (idx 10/11), upsert_role appended (?11/?12). ENFORCEMENT added in api.rs::upload_file: effective = server master AND role cap — this ALSO closes a latent gap (image_sharing_enabled/file_sharing_enabled were settable but NEVER enforced server-side pre-v0.261, only the chat UI hid the button). Roles grid + add-custom-role form gained Image/File checkbox columns. 3 roles tests (seed-on/roundtrip/default-deny) + relay+native+3 lints green. roles-system.md updated; R4 now reclassified IN-PROGRESS as v0.262 Phase 2.
recent_decisions.rationale: Finished the interrupted Phase 1 cleanly before touching Phase 2 (do not leave a data-model change half-done or lose it). Non-breaking migration (DEFAULT 1 everywhere) is mandatory for a live server. Enforcing the previously-decorative server toggle is the no-bandaid bonus.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.260: removed the temporary layout-dbg readout from tinted_section. Operator said "looking better" + moved to feature questions, did not pursue centering — so the shipped debug clutter (3 lines, every section, all users) gets removed rather than left in production. Centering remains 0/3 / unsolved but DE-PRIORITIZED (operator content with current left layout; revisit only if re-raised, re-instrument then). form_row label-pin (v0.259) confirmed working in operator screenshot — toggles now align. Verified in code: per-role caps (can_stream/can_upload/can_voice) ALREADY exist + are edited in the Roles grid; the Add-a-custom-role form exists at draw_roles_admin bottom (operator screenshot cut off at Donor so Moderator/Admin/add-form were below the fold → discoverability gap, not a missing feature). image/file sharing are the only server-wide-ONLY settings (no per-role cap) — the real inconsistency behind the operator combine-into-grid instinct.
recent_decisions.rationale: Shipping temporary debug UI then leaving it is sloppy; cleaned up since the operator deprioritized centering. The roles editor exists — the problem is discoverability + a fragmented 3-block per-role model; that and the per-role-image/file idea are a data-model decision to confirm with the operator, not assume.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.259: (1) Universal form_row fix — form_row_inner allocate_ui_with_layout label cell had NO set_min_width(label_width), so it collapsed to text width and the control started at a variable x per row → toggles/inputs stairstepped (operator: sharing & policy toggles vs the others). Added ui.set_min_width(label_width) — exact kv_row v0.254 fix, now on the universal widget → aligns EVERY form_row app-wide (settings/ai_usage/onboarding/server_settings). (2) Centering still failed on the v0.258.1 screenshot (3rd miss: vertical_centered+set_max_width, allocate_ui_with_layout, new_child+max_rect all rendered full-bleed-left). Stopped guessing — added a TEMPORARY on-screen layout-dbg readout in tinted_section (avail / col / x0 / childAW) so the operator next screenshot yields the real runtime numbers (esp. childAW: if ≫ col then new_child(max_rect) is not hard-constraining = the actual root cause). Data-driven next iteration instead of attempt 4 blind.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator pointed at the toggles; fixed precisely + universally (high confidence, proven pattern). Was candid that centering is 0/3 and I cannot solve it blind; instrumenting converts the working screenshot loop into a one-shot diagnosis instead of burning more build cycles theorizing about egui.

recent_decisions.decision: Server Settings centering attempt 3 (v0.258) — the one that should actually work. v0.253 vertical_centered+set_max_width and v0.257 horizontal+add_space+allocate_ui_with_layout BOTH failed (operator screenshot on v0.257.1 confirmed section still full-bleed-left). Root cause finally pinned: inside ScrollArea::auto_shrink([false,false]) set_max_width is a soft wrapping hint and allocate_ui_with_layout size is ambiguous, so ui.separator()/Grid/hint expand to the full viewport. Fix: ui.new_child(egui::UiBuilder::new().max_rect(centered_rect).layout(top_down)) — a child UI with an EXPLICIT max_rect; available_width() then derives from that rect so ALL children (separators included) are hard-bounded. Exact primitive already proven in widgets/body_pill.rs. Added ui.allocate_rect(child.min_rect(),hover) so the parent cursor advances (sections still stack). Also de-cramped the policy matrix + roles grids (column spacing spacing_lg/md -> spacing_xl, min_col_width on the matrix). Was candid with operator: this is attempt 3, using the codebase-proven primitive not more theory; asked for one more screenshot.
recent_decisions.rationale: Shipped two wrong width fixes by theorizing about egui instead of using the unambiguous primitive. Stopped guessing, grepped the codebase for a demonstrably-working pattern (body_pill new_child+max_rect), used exactly that. Honesty about the two misses + the collaborative screenshot loop is the candor the operator values; visual correctness can only be verified by him on the build.

recent_decisions.decision: Server Settings styling 4/N (v0.257): operator asked center-align everything in the settings menu. Pushed back candidly: literal per-element centering wrecks scannability for forms + the new data grids (eye scans down a consistent left edge; ragged centered rows force re-finding each line start). Delivered the synthesis instead — fixed tinted_section so it ACTUALLY constrains+centers (the old vertical_centered+set_max_width does not clamp inside a vertical ScrollArea, so sections blew out to ~1900px and content was stranded left). New: explicit side-padded fixed-width column (avail.min(max_width), push in by half the slack, pin inner ui min+max width so the Frame cannot expand). Result: each section is a centered ~960 readable column, content stays LEFT-aligned inside (scannable). Zero blast radius — tinted_section is used ONLY by server_settings.rs.
recent_decisions.rationale: Memory user_design_intuition: investigate his hunches, do not casually dismiss. The hunch (content stranded left in a giant empty box looks bad) is correct; the proposed mechanism (center every element) is the wrong lever for a forms/tables page. Candor memory: be a real friend, push back when I disagree — explained the scannability cost and shipped the standard centered-readable-column pattern (GitHub/macOS/Discord settings) that gives him what he actually wants without the cost. Visual call best judged by him on a current build.

recent_decisions.decision: Server Settings styling 3/N (v0.256): fixed the roles-row stairstep (operator #1 uneven rows). Was a per-role ui.horizontal inline flow — variable-width name (Unverified vs Moderator) + combo-vs-label tier segment made every Save/Delete land at a different x. Rebuilt as one egui::Grid (8 cols: Role | Color | Stream | Upload | Voice | Tier | Save | Delete), header row, built-ins emit a placeholder Delete cell so striped rows stay rectangular. Capability checkboxes lost inline labels (header names them). Same proven Grid technique as the v0.255 policy matrix. ADMIN screenshots were on v0.253.1 so operator has not yet seen v0.254 (button/kv) or v0.255 (policy matrix) — flagged repeatedly to update to v0.256.1.
recent_decisions.rationale: Completes the operator two stated complaints (#2 policy v0.255, #1 roles v0.256) with the same low-risk idiomatic egui::Grid primitive. Channels grid is internally fine (shared widths) — the remaining gridfill-width is cosmetic/optional and lower priority; stopping the per-release dribble after #1+#2 are both done is the right checkpoint to let the operator eyeball on a current build.

recent_decisions.decision: Server Settings styling 2/N (v0.255): rebuilt the Server-policy section. Was ~20 widgets::form_row rows whose long "Max X — tier (default N)" labels wrapped to 3 LINES in form_row 140px column, tiny int beside, ~70% row empty (operator #2: two skinny ¼-width columns + ugly triple-wrap, confirmed by ADMIN screenshots). Replaced the 12 per-role numeric form_rows with one egui::Grid tier×metric MATRIX (rows=Unverified/Verified/Moderator/Admin, cols=Max chars / Max upload MB / Uploads kept) — auto-aligned columns by construction (no fixed-px), defaults moved to a single hint so cells stay clean; total-cap + toggles + PQ-hint + extensions kept but regrouped under a Sharing-toggles subsection. Note: operator was on v0.253.1 so v0.254 button/kv fixes not yet visible to them — flagged to update.
recent_decisions.rationale: Picked the worst, most-contained, most-confirmed offender (#2 policy) for a clean standalone ship; egui::Grid is the idiomatic aligned-table primitive (matches ui-system.md no-magic-numbers — columns auto-size, theme-token spacing). Shipped incrementally so the operator can eyeball the matrix before the same Grid technique is applied to the roles-row stairstep (#1). Channels grid is internally fine (shared widths) — lower priority than roles.

recent_decisions.decision: Server Settings styling arc started (operator: page poorly styled, uneven grids, policy = two skinny ¼-width columns). Screenshot CORRECTED my static read: sections render full-width, not 960-capped — the dominant visible defects are (1) Button::Secondary = transparent fill + #2a2a35 border → invisible on dark/tinted panels, every secondary button app-wide rendered as bare text; (2) kv_row/kv_row_with_definition (server_settings.rs) hand-roll a label column via allocate_ui_with_layout WITHOUT set_min_width + no gutter → column can collapse, hardcoded 160 not the token. v0.254 fixes BOTH at the universal layer: button.rs Secondary now fill=bg_tertiary + text_muted hairline (raised-surface, app-wide one-change win); added the missing pub fn bg_tertiary() accessor (token existed, no accessor); kv_row pinned with set_min_width(theme.settings_label_width) + spacing_md gutter, token not magic 160. Still pending (operator stated #1/#2): 5 ad-hoc fixed-px grids (channels/banned/muted/roles/members) → one shared themed weighted-width data_table; policy form rows not filling width.
recent_decisions.rationale: Picked the two highest-impact, lowest-risk, universal-layer fixes first — the secondary-button defect lifts the WHOLE app (ui-system.md one-source-of-truth), kv_row hardening is token-correct + cheap. Shipping incrementally so the operator sees the dramatic button improvement live before the bigger (riskier) 5-grid data_table refactor. Owned that the screenshot corrected my first (width-cap) theory — trust-but-verify worked.

recent_decisions.decision: PQ Increment 3 SHIPPED (v0.253) — gated PQ-signature ENFORCEMENT. New server_settings.require_pq_signatures bool (default OFF; full positional-SQL wiring: struct/Default/get ?22/set ?23/CREATE+idempotent ALTER; round-trip test locks it). Relay Chat handler: Inc2 soft-log replaced with a (has_pq_key, sent_pq_sig, require_pq) match — PQ key+valid=OK; PQ key+invalid/missing & require ON = REJECT (Private actionable error + continue, no broadcast); require OFF = Inc2 soft; NO pq key on file = NEVER enforced (the safety valve — old/incapable clients never lock out, auto-upgrade on reconnect). ServerSettingsUpdate gained require_pq_signatures Option<bool> + handler apply. Native Server Settings ADMIN policy got the checkbox + a thorough body_hint (turn on after pq_dualsign telemetry shows full adoption; reversible).
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator: Do it, get accounts off the backlog. Candid engineering call: "make PQ canonical" done SAFELY = enforce the PQ sig (the actual quantum-forgery defense), NOT re-key every Ed25519-keyed table (catastrophic, orphans data, never right at billions-scale). Ed25519 stays the storage key + Solana wallet; the existing Ed25519<->Dilithium map + an enforcement gate IS the correct identity-migration pattern (resolution indirection, not primary-key rewrite). Toggle default-OFF + never-enforce-uncapable-accounts makes the cutover a safe, reversible, informed 1-click op instead of a deploy-day lockout. Pushed back on the implicit big-bang while delivering the real security goal.

recent_decisions.decision:
PQ Increment 2 SHIPPED (v0.252) — dual-sign, soft/log-only. Chat messages now carry pq_signature (Dilithium3 sig over the IDENTICAL preimage as Ed25519: content
timestamp). crypto.js pqSignChatMessage; 4 chat-messages.js send sites attach it. Relay Chat handler extracts pq_signature from the raw inbound JSON Value (NO RelayMessage::Chat enum/constructor change — avoided the ~20-site ripple; soft mode does not persist/rebroadcast it), and when the sender dilithium_public is on file, verify_dilithium_signature (new, broadcast.rs, mirrors verify_ed25519_signature, same preimage) → tracing target pq_dualsign PQ-OK/PQ-MISMATCH. Ed25519 stays authoritative; PQ result NEVER rejects/alters broadcast. New cross-impl KAT: dilithium_js_signature_verifies_in_rust embeds a frozen noble-produced 3309-byte sig fixture (src/relay/core/pq_kat_dilithium_sig.hex) and asserts RustCrypto verify_dilithium accepts it + rejects tampered (ML-DSA sign is hedged/non-deterministic so we freeze a valid sig; verify is deterministic). 12 pq_crypto tests + JS pq-kat + 3 lints + native/relay all green.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator: do what you believe best (3rd autonomy grant) + wants PQ momentum. Inc2 is safe/additive (soft verify = zero enforcement = zero break even if a client preimage were wrong — just telemetry). Extracting pq_signature from raw JSON instead of typing it into the enum was the key scope/risk decision: Chat is constructed in ~20 places; a typed field would force editing them all. STOPPING after Inc2 — Inc3 (canonical flip + Ed25519->PQ alias/migration so no data orphaned) is the catastrophic-if-wrong step and explicitly needs operator sign-off + its own session.

recent_decisions.decision: PQ migration Increment 1 SHIPPED (v0.251) — additive dual-key foundation. Chat client now derives a Dilithium3 keypair from the SAME 32-byte BIP39 seed as Ed25519 (web/chat/pq.js + attachPqIdentity in crypto.js) and presents dilithium_public on the identify message; relay records it in nullable registered_names.dilithium_public alongside Ed25519. Ed25519 stays the canonical primary key — NOTHING canonical changed. Mirrors the ecdh_public precedent end-to-end (migration/storage/enum/handler/Peer/PeerInfo/UserInfo/PeerJoined). CRITICAL correctness work: discovered noble@2.x blake3 derive-key context must be UTF-8 BYTES not a string (existing pq-identity.js passes a string → that federation PQ path was never exercised / is broken) AND noble@0.6.x ml-dsa API is sign(msg,sk)/verify(sig,msg,pk) (pq-identity.js has the old wrong order too). Established a cross-language KAT: fixed master 32x0x07 -> dil_seed f0dfc6e8.. -> pk blake3 3f4ff5c7.. , asserted in BOTH src/relay/core/pq_crypto.rs::dilithium_cross_language_kat AND scripts/pq-kat.mjs (just pq-kat) against the actual vendored artifact. Verified noble ml-dsa-65 == RustCrypto ml-dsa byte-for-byte. noble VENDORED same-origin (web/shared/vendor/noble-pq.bundle.js, 58KB self-contained ESM, provenance header + sha256, reproducible via scripts/build-noble-bundle.mjs / just pq-vendor) — no CDN for a primary-identity dep; CSP script-src self already covers it. Graceful fallback everywhere (no PQ -> exact pre-PQ Ed25519 behavior). Storage round-trip test added. cargo native+relay + 3 lint tests + both KATs green.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator: go full PQ, do it right from the start. Pushed back candidly that the canonical flip must be per-user-as-they-reconnect (clients update asynchronously; everything keyed by Ed25519 hex) — so it ships across several dependency-ordered releases, Inc1 being the mandatory additive foundation (builds the Ed25519->Dilithium map). Vendoring (vs CDN allowlist) chosen because a primary-identity crypto dep must not depend on a third party — that IS doing it right. KAT-locked cross-language because a silent JS/Rust derivation mismatch = unrecoverable identity corruption.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.250: 6 handler-layer tests for handle_mod_action (src/relay/handlers/msg_handlers.rs mod_action_tests). RelayState::new(db) needs only a Storage + no network, so the async handler is fully testable; tests subscribe to broadcast_tx to capture Private/System feedback and drive the async fn via a manual tokio Runtime (tokio has rt-multi-thread but not the macros feature, so no #[tokio::test]). Coverage: non-mod refused; ban admin-only gate; admin-ban persists+captures name (v0.245 trap guard end-to-end); NAME-ONLY kick of an admin refused (the exact v0.247 vuln); self-ban-by-name blocked; mute preserves role through the handler (v0.246 invariant). Operator chose Not-now-keep-building for the PQ migration, so continuing lower-priority safe autonomous work.
recent_decisions.rationale: Storage tests (v0.249) covered the data layer; the async handler logic (permission gates, candidate-key resolution, the v0.247 name-only protection, v0.246 role-preservation) was still untested. These are the highest-value zero-risk tests available and directly lock the 3 moderation/security changes shipped this session.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.249: added 9 storage regression tests (src/relay/storage/channels.rs ban_mute_tests) locking the v0.245/246/247 invariants — ban captures name (irreversible-ban-trap guard), set_banned back-compat, empty-key no-op, re-ban replaces not duplicates, MUTE-DOES-NOT-CLOBBER-ROLE (the core v0.246 fix), legacy role=muted cleared to unverified on unmute, unmute role-safe for non-muted, ban/mute independence. Followed the existing dms.rs fresh_db() Storage::open temp-file pattern. Also VERIFIED + corrected a stale journal gap: the 5 Tier-3 infinite-of-x items were already migrated in v0.133 (loaders+data+fetch all present); only calculator buttons remain and those are explicitly acceptable.
recent_decisions.rationale: Just shipped 3 moderation/security changes (v0.245-247) into msg_handlers/storage with ZERO automated coverage; the journal also lists msg_handlers test coverage as a pending audit item. Storage-layer tests are zero-risk, need no network harness, and pin the exact design decisions (esp. mute-orthogonal-to-role) so a future refactor can not silently reintroduce the role-clobber data-loss bug. Deliberately did NOT start the PQ chat migration autonomously — foundational identity/crypto change, operator-stated-direction-not-yet-shipped, high blast radius (auto-mode caution).

recent_decisions.decision: Cosmos Phase 4d-quad shipped (v0.248): (1) eclipse shadow-axis ground point — subpoint_lat_lon_deg() converts the Earth->Moon ecliptic vector to geographic lat/lon using J2000 mean obliquity (23.4393 deg physical constant) + the IAU GMST polynomial; both exact in the model J2000 time base. Honest approximation (sub-lunar point, ignores parallax/oblateness/dT) — labeled approx in the HUD. (2) Forward Sky-Events scan — scan_upcoming_sky_events() steps sim_time 3h for 400 days, detecting eclipses by enter/exit windows (refined to 1-min peak) and tight (<1deg) conjunctions/occultations by per-pair local-minimum-separation turning points (refined). Cached in GuiState cosmos_upcoming_* with a 12h-drift + 300ms-wall throttle so the ~3000-sample scan never runs per-frame. HUD: eclipse readout gains a shadow-axis-over-~LAT/LON line; Sky-events box gains an upcoming section (in Xd (date) label, severity color). Validated by 3 unit tests incl. sub-solar latitude must hit +/-23.44 over a year (pins obliquity sign+magnitude; celestial pole P=(0,cose,sine) derived for the model Y-up/XZ-ecliptic frame).
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list item; continues the in-flight Cosmos 4d arc (4d-tri instant detector explicitly TODO-ed 4d-quad). Chose this over PQ chat migration: PQ is a foundational identity/security migration the operator stated as direction-not-yet-shipped — too high blast radius to do autonomously without operator involvement (auto-mode caution rule). 4d-quad is self-contained, math-validated, zero security surface.

recent_decisions.decision: Closed the name-only-kick admin-protection security gap (v0.247). handle_mod_action previously only ran the "non-admin cannot act on an admin" check when an explicit target KEY was supplied; the name-only path (target empty, target_name set) skipped it entirely — a rogue mod could kick/ban an admin by name. Fix: resolve candidate_keys = [target key if any] + keys_for_name(target_name) (dedup), then run BOTH protections against the whole set: (a) self-action guard now catches name-only self-target (and extended to mute), (b) admin/owner protection refuses a non-admin if ANY resolved registration is admin/owner. Also widened protected set from admin-only to admin OR owner.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list security item. The TODO had said "currently fine because only admins use moderation UI in practice" — that is a trust assumption, not a control. Mission = civilization-scale, billions of users, AI agents as mods; the control must hold without assuming who uses the UI. Self-mute now blocked too (footgun: a mod muting themselves has no easy modal self-recovery).

recent_decisions.decision: Mute reworked off the role bandaid (v0.246). The OLD /mute (broadcast.rs handle_mod_command) set user_roles.role=muted — clobbering the real role (donor/verified/mod) — and /unmute reset role=user (a non-existent role -> default-denies to unverified). Data loss: mute+unmute a Donor and they came back unverified. The modal Mute (mod_action mute) was outright unimplemented (returned not-implemented-use-kick). Built a muted_members table ORTHOGONAL to roles: is_muted/mute_user/unmute_user/list_muted + MutedUser struct (mirrors banned_keys/BannedUser). unmute_user also clears any LEGACY role=muted row, best-effort reset to unverified (their original role was already lost by the old bandaid). relay.rs Chat handler now gates on role==muted OR is_muted() (legacy honored). New WS: MutedListRequest/MutedList{target}/Unmute (mod-gated; MutedList targeted via send-loop filter, never leaks). mod_action mute|unmute implemented (multi-key via keys_for_name, notifies actor + target). Slash /mute /unmute now call mute_user/unmute_user (no role clobber). New MOD-section Server Settings > Muted users panel (per-row Unmute, mirrors Banned users). Mute stays mod-level; the existing admin-protection check (any action on an admin by non-admin is refused) already covers mute-an-admin.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list item: muted_members table for Mute. Doing it as a separate table (not role=muted) is the no-bandaid choice — mute must not destroy role state. Mirrored the v0.245 ban architecture 1:1 for consistency (same panel pattern, same targeted-WS-delivery, same chrono-free date fmt).

recent_decisions.decision: Full ban management (v0.245). The modal Ban button (handle_mod_action) never persisted to banned_keys — Ban silently == Kick. Wired it: ban_user(key,name) inserts into banned_keys, force-closes live sockets (kicked_keys + peers.remove for BOTH kick and ban now), and the identify gate (relay.rs:2320, already existed) rejects banned keys on reconnect. CRITICAL discovery: the kick path deletes the user from registered_names, so /unban <name> (resolves via keys_for_name) could no longer find a key — a ban was IRREVERSIBLE. Fix per no-bandaids rule: banned_keys gained a name column (idempotent ALTER + fresh CREATE), new storage BannedUser struct + list_banned()/ban_user()/unban_user() (set_banned kept as name-less delegator for slash /ban /unban back-comat). New WS: BannedListRequest{} (admin-gated), BannedList{users,target:Option} (targeted to requesting admin via send-loop filter so the ban list never broadcasts to non-admins), Unban{target}. New Server Settings > ADMIN > Banned users panel: auto-requests once/session, Refresh button, per-row name/short-key/date + Unban. chat_banned_users + chat_banned_requested GuiState fields; chat_banned_requested reset on ws disconnect. Slash /ban now uses ban_user too. Past-tense verb fix ("baned"->"Banned").
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator priority list item "banned_keys wire-up so Ban prevents rejoin". A moderation feature that silently half-works is exactly the kind of bandaid the mission doc forbids. Built the full reversible loop (ban persists + unban UI) rather than just the insert, because a ban with no working unban is irreversible-by-accident.

recent_decisions.decision: Data-driven roles system (docs/design/roles-system.md). New `roles` SQLite table seeded with 5 built-ins (unverified/verified/donor/mod/admin); each role has can_stream/can_upload/can_voice capabilities + a base_tier that inherits the existing server_settings per-tier numeric limits (avoids a big limit migration; R4 consolidates later). Capability composition: effective_can_X = server_master_X_toggle && role.can_X. Assignment via the chat user-modal Role dropdown (set_user_role WS, generalizes mod_action mod/unmod). Built-in roles: caps editable, id/trust/base_tier locked, undeletable. Shipped R1 (v0.239 backend), R2 (v0.241 client parse + dropdown + data-driven badges), R3 (v0.242 Server Settings roles editor). R4 (move per-role limits into the roles table, deprecate base_tier) deferred until per-custom-role limits are actually wanted.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator wanted custom ranks + 'my lil bro big bro dad etc should be able to stream to our vps relay' WITHOUT promoting them to mod/admin. Chose (via AskUserQuestion) fully data-driven roles + per-role stream/upload/voice + role-dropdown assignment. Per infinite-of-x: roles are data not code. BEHAVIOR CHANGE shipped in v0.239: admin streaming now also requires the server-wide video_streaming_enabled master toggle (default OFF; old start path ignored it) — operator was told to flip it on in Server policy.

recent_decisions.decision: Clipboard image paste (Ctrl+V) MUST be detected at the raw winit layer (src/lib.rs window_event), NOT through egui's input. egui-winit intercepts the Cut/Copy/Paste shortcuts, reads clipboard TEXT only, and returns early WITHOUT emitting the key event — so for an image-only clipboard egui's input has zero signal. Took 3 attempts (v0.232 post-TextEdit, v0.233 top-of-draw — both read egui input, both failed; v0.234 winit-layer flag — works). The bug class is confined to exactly Cut/Copy/Paste; audited all of src/, no other instances. Future shortcut handling that competes with egui-winit's swallowed shortcuts must use the winit layer.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator: print-screen then Ctrl+V worked on web but not the native app. The architectural lesson (egui-winit swallows C/X/V) is the durable takeaway — captured in lib.rs comments + this journal so it isn't rediscovered.

recent_decisions.decision: Split items toxicology: ingredients hold their own tox in sidecar RON files; items are pure ingredient lists with versioned `history` array. Toxicology derived at query time from the current ingredient list.
recent_decisions.rationale: User asked: 'wouldn't it be better to just list the item and ingredients and then from that list of ingredients determines toxicology' + 'we also want to make sure we accommodate the fact that the ingredients list might change for something like Oreos.' Both improvements landed in v0.116.1.

recent_decisions.decision: Multi-AI coordination = static registry (data/coordination/agent_registry.ron) + runtime claim table (agent_sessions SQLite) + audit JSON files (data/coordination/sessions/*.json) + aggregator (node scripts/agent-status.js).
recent_decisions.rationale: User wanted a way for fresh AI sessions to check if a scope already has work in progress, see completion estimates, and know if their assigned scope is at a stop state.

recent_decisions.decision: Going forward, ALWAYS git-add untracked .rs/.ron/.csv before pushing. Saved as feedback_untracked_files.md.
recent_decisions.rationale: v0.108 created issuer_trust.rs but never staged it. CI broke 8 releases later when desktop-build CI tried fresh checkout.

Audit found federation.rs:220 + federation.rs:461 had open TODOs for client signature verification. Forward-compatible verifier landed without breaking current unsigned-client federation. Closes Tier 1.1 of the audit punch list.

recent_decisions.decision: Doc sweep landed in v0.122.0: SOP.md/STATUS.md/CONTRIBUTING.md/SYNC.md/SELF-HOSTING.md/ONBOARDING.md/Justfile/4 design docs all rewritten to drop dead `server/`, `crates/`, `native/`, `app/` paths and the dead `humanity-relay` package name.
recent_decisions.rationale: Audit Tier 2 — these docs were misleading future contributors and AI agents who'd grep for paths that no longer exist. CLAUDE.md/README/FEATURES were already correct; the rest had drifted. STATUS.md flipped 16 scaffolded systems + electrical + psychology from ✅ to ⚠️ — the 'truthfulness rule' is now stated explicitly in the header.

recent_decisions.decision: `just theme` recipe added; runs `node scripts/gen-theme-css.js` to regenerate `web/shared/theme.css` from `data/gui/theme.ron`. Wired into `just ship` so push-time always regenerates. Idempotent.
recent_decisions.rationale: Audit Tier 5 — script existed but wasn't wired into any build step, so theme.css drift was inevitable. Closes that gap.

recent_decisions.decision: Audit Session 2 (Tier 3) landed in v0.123.0. Seven hardcoded arrays migrated to data files: `data/inventory/equipment_slots.json`, `data/bugs/taxonomy.json`, `data/crafting/categories.json`, `data/market/categories.json`, `data/resources/catalog.json`, `data/studio/{scenes,sources}.json`, `data/themes/presets.json`. New `pub fn load_*` helpers in `src/gui/mod.rs` populate `GuiState` at startup; pages read from `state.X`. JS theme presets regenerated by `scripts/gen-theme-presets-js.js`, wired into `just theme`.
recent_decisions.rationale: Audit Tier 3 (Infinite-of-X) — design rule says 'anything that can exist more than once is a data file, not code'. The migrations close the 8 hardcoded violations the audit found in `docs/design/infinite-of-x.md`. Pattern: top-level `load_*(data_dir)` returning owned Vec, graceful fallback to empty Vec on missing/malformed input — pages render an empty filter row rather than crashing. Type-renamed legacy `Resource`/`ResourceCategory` (resources.rs-private, `&'static str`) into owned `ResourceEntry`/`ResourceCategory` in `gui/mod.rs`. Studio types kept; new `StudioScenePreset`/`StudioSourcePreset` deserialise from JSON and convert to `StudioScene`/`StudioSource` via `studio_scene_from_preset`/`studio_source_from_preset`.

recent_decisions.decision: BUG-034 fixed in v0.124.0. `.github/workflows/build-desktop.yml` now publishes raw binaries (`HumanityOS-<platform>.exe` for Windows, plain executable for Linux/macOS) alongside the existing `.tar.gz` bundles. `src/updater.rs::find_platform_asset` prefers raw binary assets and refuses archive-only releases (returns 'No binary for this platform') instead of silently writing gzip bytes as a corrupt exe.
recent_decisions.rationale: User reported in-app updater on Windows produced a binary that failed to launch with 'Unsupported 16-Bit Application'. Investigation: corrupted file was 38612146 bytes — exactly the size of `HumanityOS-windows-x64.tar.gz`. The updater downloaded the only release asset (a tar.gz bundle) and renamed it to .exe without extracting. `file` on the corrupted exe confirms gzip magic bytes. Two-part fix: workflow ships both formats; updater prefers raw.

recent_decisions.decision: Audit Session 3 (Tier 1.2 + Tier 4) lands alongside the BUG-034 fix in v0.124.0. Storage tests added: 7 for `dms.rs` (DM persistence, E2EE ciphertext preservation, direction-symmetric load, conversation isolation, mark-read, ECDH requires registered name, limit returns recent N) and 10 for `trading.rs` (escrow happy-path, complete requires both confirms, complete idempotent, item changes reset confirmations, cancelled cannot complete, third-party cannot confirm, cannot confirm pending, rejected→cancelled, unknown side rejected, list filters by party). Total lib tests: 173 → 190. Tier 4 dual-UI parity table added to `docs/design/ui-system.md`.
recent_decisions.rationale: Audit Tier 1.2 — msg_handlers.rs et al had 0 tests over 3081 LOC. Storage-layer tests are the right scope (handler tests would need a heavy RelayState fixture). The new tests cover the load-bearing escrow correctness invariants the audit flagged.

recent_decisions.decision: BUG-035 + BUG-036 fixed in v0.125.0. (035) Native chat reply disappears after WS blip — fixed by no-op'ing channel-switch when the click target equals the active channel (chat.rs, 5 sites) and adding chat_sent_timestamps dedup to the HTTP history fetch (lib.rs). (036) Deleted system channels resurrect on every relay restart — fixed by gating the seed list with a `default_channels_seeded` row in server_state, with a one-shot migration that respects existing operators' channel sets when message_count > 0.
recent_decisions.rationale: User reported a reply going missing in #general after a connection blip, plus channel-deletes via the cog modal coming back. Investigation: chat_messages.clear() ran on every click (even same-channel re-clicks); the HTTP history fetch had no dedup; src/relay/mod.rs re-seeded 6 system channels on every restart and INSERT OR IGNORE happily re-inserted previously-deleted rows. Both bugs documented in docs/BUGS.md as BUG-035 and BUG-036 with full root-cause traces.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.126.0 reduces the default channel seed to `general` + `announcements` only. Welcome/Rules content snapshotted into `data/server/legacy_channel_content.json` for the future /welcome and /rules page migration. The `stream` and `dev` channels are dropped outright. Live VPS legacy channels (welcome/rules/stream/dev) deleted via SQL.
recent_decisions.rationale: User feedback: 'welcome and rules channels should be handled by HOS, not channels. Like proper pages dedicated to this stuff that we can easily update'. Channels are the wrong shape for static reference content; pages backed by data files are correct. v0.127.0 will build the actual /welcome /rules /accord pages.

recent_decisions.decision: Distribution-sovereignty plan documented in `docs/distribution-mirrors.md`. Recommended adoption order: (1) self-hosted Forgejo on VPS, (2) Codeberg mirror, (3) VPS release-binary mirror, (4) self-hosted BitTorrent tracker + seeder for data/asset payload, (5) Internet Archive seeder, (6) Software Heritage archival, (7) WinGet manifest. Steps 8+ scope-driven (IPFS, F-Droid/Flathub/Snap, Homebrew, Radicle, paid IPFS pinning).
recent_decisions.rationale: User wants HOS independent of GitHub, prefers self-hosting + torrent/magnet links to fit the P2P federation ethos. Vision: small exe is the entry point; on first run it pulls data/+assets/ from the torrent network with VPS as always-on seeder; client devices become seeders too. The doc is curated — only platforms that are good fits are listed. GitLab, SourceForge, Bitbucket, NotABug, Chocolatey were considered and dropped.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 1 of distribution-sovereignty plan landed in v0.127.0: self-hosted Forgejo at https://git.united-humanity.us/shaostoul/humanity. Forgejo v15.0.0 binary at /usr/local/bin/forgejo; user `forgejo`; data at /var/lib/forgejo; SQLite3; nginx reverse proxy 127.0.0.1:3000; Let's Encrypt TLS auto-renewing; self-registration disabled. Justfile `_commit` recipe now pushes to BOTH `origin` (GitHub) and `forge` (Forgejo); forge push is best-effort (`-` prefix in just) so a transient Forgejo outage doesn't block ship. Tags pushed to both remotes too (GitHub Actions Build Desktop App still keys off origin tag). Initial mirror: 12386 objects, 50 tags (v0.1.0 → v0.126.0), 2 branches (main + claude/happy-panini), 51 MB. `docs/forgejo-setup.md` documents the install + reproduce steps.
recent_decisions.rationale: Sovereignty layer is the keystone of the distribution-mirrors plan. Once forge holds the source, subsequent steps (release-binary mirror, BitTorrent seeder, Internet Archive backup, etc.) build on it. Two issues hit during install: Debian 11's git 2.30.2 too old for Forgejo (needed ≥2.34.1) — fixed by building 2.45.2 from source at /usr/local/bin/git; app.ini owned root:forgejo prevented the installer from saving config — fixed by chown forgejo:forgejo. Both documented in forgejo-setup.md so future operators don't repeat them.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 3 of distribution-sovereignty plan landed in v0.128.0: self-hosted release-binary mirror at https://united-humanity.us/releases/. nginx /releases/ location with autoindex; backfilled v0.122.0–v0.127.0; build-desktop.yml CI scp's binaries to VPS on every tag push via appleboy/scp-action; mirror-manifest job regenerates manifest.json + latest symlink via /usr/local/bin/regen-releases-manifest. Step 2 (Codeberg) skipped temporarily — operator awaiting signup confirmation email; will land in a follow-up release.
recent_decisions.rationale: The auto-updater (BUG-034 fix in v0.124.0) will gain this as a fallback URL later — for now the mirror is a working independent download path, anyone can wget from it without GitHub. The manifest at /releases/manifest.json + the latest symlink mean operators don't need to know specific version numbers; clients can fetch latest deterministically. nginx sites-enabled/humanity was a regular file (not a symlink to sites-available) — edits had to go directly to sites-enabled. Documented inline in the orchestrator notes.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.128.2 shipped a CI fix: per-matrix mirror step replaced with a single Linux mirror-to-vps job using actions/download-artifact + appleboy/scp-action. v0.128.3 followed with continue-on-error on the upload step + if: always() on the regen step, because scp-action sometimes returns a false-positive i/o timeout AFTER the upload completes successfully. Workflow is now self-healing.
recent_decisions.rationale: Original v0.128.0 workflow used appleboy/scp-action per-matrix-job, but Docker container actions only run on Linux runners — macOS/Windows runners failed with 'Container action is only supported on Linux'. v0.128.2 consolidated the upload into one Linux job. v0.128.3 added tolerance for the action's tendency to return false-positive timeouts after successful uploads. Both fixes verified end-to-end in CI.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 4 of distribution-sovereignty plan landed in v0.129.0: BitTorrent seeder + magnet URIs. transmission-daemon installed on VPS (RPC localhost-only, peer port 51413/tcp+udp). /usr/local/bin/torrent-create-and-seed script creates .torrent files with 5 public trackers + WebSeed pointing back at the HTTPS mirror, then adds them to transmission. /usr/local/bin/regen-releases-manifest extended to a two-phase script: (1) seed any unseeded .tar.gz, (2) build manifest with magnet URIs. Backfilled 36 torrents covering v0.122.0–v0.128.3 (~1.4 GB seeding). docs/torrent-infrastructure.md documents the full setup.
recent_decisions.rationale: Matches the user's original P2P architecture vision: VPS as always-on seed; client devices become seeders too via swarm participation. WebSeed makes the critical path tracker-independent — even with zero peers and every public tracker down, BitTorrent clients fall back to plain HTTPS from the VPS. CI required NO workflow change because the regen script now handles both seeding and manifest generation. Self-hosted tracker (opentracker) deferred — not blocking because of WebSeed.

recent_decisions.decision: Step 4.5 landed in v0.130.0: layered packages + file-level manifest. New scripts/gen-data-manifest.js walks data/ + assets/icons/ + assets/shaders/, writes data-manifest-<tag>.json with per-file SHA-256 hash + Forgejo per-file URL (https://git.united-humanity.us/shaostoul/humanity/raw/tag/<tag>/<path>). bundle-data CI job now generates the manifest + uploads it as a release asset + as a workflow artifact. mirror-to-vps job depends on bundle-data so it picks up the data archive + manifest via download-artifact. Backfilled 10 data archives + 10 data manifests for v0.122.0–v0.129.0. transmission auto-seeded 10 new data-archive torrents (50 total now, ~3.6 GB).
recent_decisions.rationale: User instinct: 'shipping 38 MB for a 4 KB recipe edit is wasteful, and we should be able to inspect what's changing'. The layered architecture (binary / data / assets as separate torrents) plus file-level manifest enables future delta sync: a 4 KB data tweak becomes a 4 KB update. Forgejo's raw-file URLs serve as the per-file CDN — no IPFS or extra storage layer required. SHA-256 chosen over BLAKE3 to avoid extra deps in the manifest generator (Node stdlib only). Client-side delta sync UI (Step 4.6+) will read this manifest, compare to local files, and fetch only what changed.

recent_decisions.decision: Gameplay loop arc shipped v0.159 → v0.172 (14 minor releases). Closes Tier 1A (game systems stub completion) + Tier 1B (chat parity) and adds a 3-quest starter gameplay loop on top. AI agents now experience the loop end-to-end against the live VPS in 8 seconds: connect → identify → join → welcome (with quest + rooms) → perceive → talk to NPC → tour rooms → explore_ship completed (+100 XP) → meet_the_crew unlocked → talk to all 6 crew → meet_the_crew completed (+200 XP, 300 total) → survey_storage unlocked. World ticks 24/7, 6 NPCs per room with dialog/greetings/wander, ambient chatter every 45s. PERSIST_KEY bumped 8 times (v1 → v8). 196 tests passing. New pages: Testing (QA loop), Browser (curated bookmarks). New nav: two-tier preview (Reality/Sim/Tools/Settings), opt-in via [▤], persists.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator was AFK and asked Claude to 'keep developing'. Each release ships independently testable changes with QA tasks added to data/testing/qa_tasks.json (now 25 entries). Quest framework is data-driven (chain_next_quest matches on quest id, easily extensible). Live VPS validation each release confirmed real-world behavior. Bot demo shows complete gameplay loop in 8 seconds — operators see the system working end-to-end without manual play.

recent_decisions.decision: Cosmos Phase 4d arc shipped v0.208 → v0.212 (5 minor releases). Closes all of the operator's explicitly-stated requests from 2026-05-12: time controls (play/pause/scrub/Now/speed presets/reverse + Howard Hinnant date conversion, no chrono dep), pill-style body labels with collision-dodging + expand-to-card, live conjunction + solar eclipse detection from Earth's POV with visual highlights + HUD readout, Lagrange-point overlay (L1-L5 for Sun-Earth/Earth-Moon/Sun-Mars/Sun-Jupiter/Sun-Saturn with notable-parking tooltips JWST/Trojans/Queqiao etc.), reference-orbit rings (LEO/MEO/GEO/HEO + Lunar TLI for Earth + areostationary/Jovo-stationary/NRHO for Mars/Jupiter/Moon). New GuiState fields: cosmos_sim_time_seconds + cosmos_sim_speed + cosmos_last_real_instant + cosmos_sim_time_initialized + cosmos_expanded_body + cosmos_show_lagrange + cosmos_show_reference_orbits. New helpers: compute_lagrange_points (cube-root-mass-ratio for L1/L2/L3 + Rodrigues for L4/L5) + detect_sky_events (pairwise angular separation O(11²)≈55 pairs per frame from Earth POV) + reference_orbits_for (const &[ReferenceOrbit] per body) + draw_reference_orbits (96-segment polylines) + draw_body_info_card (auto-positioning + auto-flip pill expand cards). Cosmos doc §17a-sexies updated to be a complete shipping log + roadmap. cosmos.rs grew from ~1500 to ~2100 lines.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator quoted: 'as close to real as possible' + 'could we add a way to watch the orbits play fast forward, rewind, and skip/seek along days/weeks/years/etc' + 'all the planets line up in real life as they are today on May 12, 2026 then we could see what it'd look like on a great conjunction' + 'experience solar eclipses on the Earth' + 'pill UI for body labels — planet sprite on left, name on right, click expands to info card' + (earlier) 'include stuff like lagrange points and ... geostationary orbit'. All explicitly named features shipped. Three remaining Phase 4 items deferred with rationale in cosmos doc: 4d-quad (umbra lat/lon + forward scan), 4b (sprite→mesh wgpu integration), 4g (FPS AR-glasses overlay, requires player-in-world).

recent_decisions.decision: Post-cutover hardening sweep CLOSED. Operator: 'How can the current system fail? Let's work through fixing the problems.' Six tasks created from the v0.266.x independent security review + the threat-model self-audit; five shipped this session, one deferred for UX. v0.274.0 Inc3b: two-phase identify with Dilithium nonce challenge — relay refuses to bind a socket to a public_key the client cannot prove possession of. Preimage `hum/identify/v1\n{nonce_hex}\n{pubkey_hex}` (UTF-8); pending_identify state struct per-socket; IdentifyChallenge/IdentifyResponse RelayMessage variants; verify_dilithium_b64 helper; inc3b_tests::identify_challenge_sign_verify_roundtrip (5 cases: happy, tampered preimage, wrong key, corrupt sig, bad b64). Bot fastpath unchanged (skips challenge, auths via bot_secret). Closes HIGH-2. v0.275.0 MED-1: 4 native chat-send sites now stamp pq_signature via net::identity::pq_sign_chat(seed, content, ts); relay's chat verify gate flipped to absent->reject for non-bot senders. Native chat no longer unsigned. v0.275.0 pq-wipe.sh hardening: Gate 1 (--yes), Gate 2 (pgrep refuses concurrent 'cargo build .. --features relay'; closes 2026-05-19 race where disk-guard rm'd target/ mid-rebuild and 888 sqlite no-such-table errors followed), Gate 3 (refuses if relay binary missing), 30s schema-readiness poll for messages+channels tables (was bare sleep 4), seed-verify count==archive-length check. v0.276.0: federation profile gossip verify_profile_signature switched Ed25519->Dilithium3. Was the last Ed25519-signed identity path that referenced the user's public_key field — which has been Dilithium hex since Inc3 — so the old verify was forgeable PQ and would have silently rejected every signed gossip post-Inc3. Test fixtures rebuilt on DilithiumKeypair::from_seed + derive_dilithium_seed; all 8 federation tests pass. sign_with_server_key (server-to-server FederationHello) kept Ed25519 by design — separate trust path, server's own key. v0.277.0: native vault PBKDF2 bumped 100_000->600_000 iters to match web (the web sandbox is the harder target; asymmetry was backwards). AppConfig + GuiState gain key_iterations: u32 (serde default 100_000 for pre-v0.277 configs). encrypt_private_key always stamps PBKDF2_ITERATIONS_NEW; decrypt_private_key takes iterations as a parameter; defensive clamp to legacy floor if iterations==0 (corruption defense). Silent migration: Unlock path re-encrypts legacy 100k vaults to 600k after the user proves they know the passphrase — one-time per vault, no UX prompt. Change-passphrase auto-upgrades by virtue of re-encrypting. 6 pbkdf2_migration_tests: new encrypts use 600k, legacy decrypts with stored iters, new round-trips, wrong iter fails, full 100k->600k round-trip, corrupt iters=0 clamps. CLAUDE.md crypto table updated for all four. Inc5c-tail (#3) deferred — 3 standalone pages (settings-app.js vault sync, admin-app.js admin_stats, market-app.js listing reviews) still Ed25519-sign relay auth; they don't share state with the chat client, so fixing requires re-prompting passphrase per page or moving features into chat shell — needs operator product call. All six security-review findings: HIGH-1 v0.267.0, HIGH-2 v0.274.0, MED-1 v0.275.0, LOW-1 documented; federation gossip + PBKDF2 are above-and-beyond closes from the threat-model self-audit.
recent_decisions.rationale: Operator asked the right meta-question ('how can it fail?') which produced the threat list. The principle: do all the easy-and-known-good fixes in one session while context is hot, so Inc6 (the attended wipe) is the only remaining gate to public launch. Inc5c-tail intentionally NOT pushed through autonomously because the UX shape is operator-product-domain — re-prompting users for their vault passphrase on three random pages would be a worse experience than the current 'broken since the cutover' state, and the right call (deprecate? fold in? re-prompt?) is the operator's. Federation gossip + PBKDF2 weren't on the review's bug list but were latent issues the audit surfaced — fixing them while in the area is the no-bandaid choice. Each task shipped as its own focused commit + tag + release + build-game so the next session sees a clean, attributable trail.

active_scopes_to_avoid: _When this list is non-empty, another orchestrator session may be coordinating those scopes; check timestamps before claiming.
version_state: local==GitHub==forge: v0.277.2 (post-cutover hardening sweep CLOSED). Native exe archived at v0.277.1 via just build-game (v0.277.2 is web-only — no rebuild needed). Releases tagged on origin: v0.274.0 (Inc3b), v0.275.0 (MED-1 + pq-wipe hardening), v0.276.0 (federation Dilithium), v0.277.0 (PBKDF2 600k), v0.277.2 (Inc5c-tail unwrapped path). v0.263.3 remains an inert misfire tag.
files_in_flight_or_recently_changed: src/gui/mod.rs (v0.123.0) — added 8 GuiState fields + 7 load_* fns + new owned types (ResourceEntry, ResourceCategory, StudioScenePreset, StudioSourcePreset) + studio_*_from_preset converters; StudioState::default() now empty (populated from presets in lib.rs)
src/lib.rs (v0.123.0) — wired 7 new load_* calls + studio preset → live state conversion at GUI startup
src/gui/pages/{inventory,bugs,crafting,market,resources}.rs (v0.123.0) — replaced hardcoded const arrays with reads from gui_state.X

scripts/gen-theme-presets-js.js (v0.123.0) — regenerates the AUTO-GENERATED block of accent/font/theme presets in web/shared/settings.js from data/themes/presets.json. Idempotent. Wired into `just theme`.
docs/design/infinite-of-x.md (v0.123.0) — checklist updated; 9 violations now Migrated, 5 remain (skills, studio config, command palette, donate FAQ, sound presets)
src/relay/handlers/federation.rs (v0.122.0) — verify_profile_signature + canonical_profile_message + should_accept_profile_gossip + 8 unit tests
Justfile (v0.122.0+v0.123.0) — `just theme` runs both gen-theme-css.js and gen-theme-presets-js.js
things_a_fresh_orchestrator_should_NOT_r: Items toxicology architecture — already separated into ingredients/ + items/ with history arrays. Don't put tox back on items.
Page parity audit — done in v0.115.0. Native + web pages aligned. Only add new pages if truly needed.
Outdated content sweep for Ed25519/Solana=identity — done in v0.116.0. New stale references would only appear in newly-added content.
Universal Button widget — built v0.113.0 + v0.114.0. Use widgets::Button, don't roll your own egui::Button.
Phase 0–8 strategic plan items — all shipped or scaffolded. See docs/STATUS.md 'Civilization Trust Layer' section.
Federation profile-signature verifier — landed in v0.122.0. Don't re-add the TODO; instead implement client-side signing when ready, then flip should_accept_profile_gossip to reject empty signatures.
Doc drift in SOP/STATUS/CONTRIBUTING/SYNC/SELF-HOSTING/ONBOARDING + 4 design docs — fixed in v0.122.0. New drift can only come from new content.

Tier 3 Infinite-of-X for 7 hardcoded arrays (inventory slots, bugs taxonomy, crafting/market categories, resources catalog, studio scenes/sources, JS theme variants) — migrated in v0.123.0. Don't re-hardcode; edit the JSON files under `data/<scope>/`.

BUG-034 (updater corrupted binary by writing tar.gz as .exe) — fixed in v0.124.0. Workflow ships both raw binaries and tar.gz bundles; updater prefers raw. The fix is forward-only: pre-v0.124.0 releases only have .tar.gz, and the updater will refuse them with 'No binary for this platform' rather than corrupting the install. Don't add a tar.gz extraction fallback — rejecting is the correct behaviour given how the workflow is now structured.

Full-PQ Inc3/Inc2b/Inc4 are DONE and shipped (v0.262.33->v0.264.1). Do NOT re-implement Dilithium-identity / Kyber-DM / ECDH-removal. The cross-language KAT + envelope tests + pq-kat.mjs prove correctness.

Do NOT wipe the prod DB unilaterally. The operator EXPLICITLY chose to run the fresh-slate wipe as the final attended Inc6 step (security-review -> deploy -> just pq-wipe yes -> operator live verify), NOT mid-cutover. pq-wipe.sh re-seeds #announcements from data/announcements_archive.json (888 msgs, validated).
Do NOT re-preserve #announcements — already exported to data/announcements_archive.json and committed (both remotes).
things_pending_or_known_gaps: Phase 6a Solana RPC: only read-only balance proxy ships; no transaction signing in relay (intentional).
Phase 6b STARK selective disclosure: scaffold + Merkle wired; full STARK verifier circuit design deferred.
Phase 7a Litestream: ops doc shipped but actually deploying replication is operator action on the VPS.
Phase 7b LoRa hardware: real radio integration deferred until hardware in hand.
PQ JS keygen: noble-PQ dynamic import landed in pq-identity.js v0.117.0; chat client rewrite still pending.
Audit Session 3 (Tier 1.2 + Tier 4): integration tests for src/relay/handlers/msg_handlers.rs (DM roundtrip, trade escrow, federation accept/reject) + dual-UI parity triage doc.
Audit Session 4+ (Tier 2.7 follow-through): implement OR document the 16 scaffolded game systems + electrical/psychology partials.

Tier 3 infinite-of-x: VERIFIED 2026-05-15 — all 5 (skills/studio-streaming/command-palette/donate-FAQ/sound-presets) ARE migrated (loaders + data/*.json + fetch wiring all present); docs/design/infinite-of-x.md v0.133 table is correct, the old gap line was stale. Only remaining: calculator.rs button labels, explicitly acceptable (pure UI layout).

Lower-priority doc drift: docs/06-SOURCE-OF-TRUTH-MAP.md FIXED v0.249.2 (dead server/crates paths -> src/relay; section-5 sub-crate guidance rewritten for single-crate reality). BUGS.md:189 + vr-tracking.md:29 are NOT drift (historical bug text / crates.io URL). Other listed docs (02-ARCHITECTURE, engine-*.md, index/README) still unaudited — sweep when convenient, low priority.
10 of 21 agent scopes still unaudited — check `node scripts/agent-status.js` for current list under 'Unaudited scopes'.
HIGH-1 FIXED v0.267.0 (web fail-closed DM send + P2P fallback; relay handle_dm rejects non-bot encrypted:false). No more silent plaintext DM.

HIGH-2 FIXED v0.274.0 (Inc3b): two-phase identify with Dilithium nonce challenge. Relay refuses to bind a socket to a public_key the client cannot prove possession of. Bot fastpath (key starts with `bot_`) bypasses challenge, auths via bot_secret.
MED-1 FIXED v0.275.0: native chat signs every chat-send site with pq_sign_chat; relay's chat verify gate flipped to absent->reject for non-bot senders. Native chat is no longer unsigned.
Federation gossip FIXED v0.276.0: verify_profile_signature switched Ed25519->Dilithium3 over `profile_v1\n...` preimage. Was forgeable PQ + would silently reject every signed gossip post-Inc3.

Native vault PBKDF2 FIXED v0.277.0: bumped 100_000->600_000 iters (matches web). AppConfig.key_iterations stamps the per-vault count; legacy 100k vaults silently re-encrypt to 600k on the next unlock (one-time per vault, no UX prompt).

pq-wipe.sh HARDENED v0.275.0: refuses concurrent cargo build (closes 2026-05-19 race where disk-guard rm'd target/ mid-rebuild), refuses if relay binary missing, polls schema readiness 30s instead of bare sleep, verifies seed count matches archive length.

Inc5c-tail UNWRAPPED PATH FIXED v0.277.2: shared helper web/shared/pq-relay-auth.js reads BIP39 seed from localStorage humanity_key_backup, derives Dilithium via pq.js, signs purpose\ntimestamp preimage. admin-app.js + settings-app.js wired. market-app.js audit-confirmed no signing call (UI-only). Wrapped-only users (passphrase-protected via wrapAndStoreKey, no plaintext backup) still see 'Sign in via Chat first' — fixing that needs a passphrase prompt in those pages OR a same-origin shared-worker for the unlocked seed; both real UX work, lower priority than launch.

Inc6 ATTENDED (operator gate): deploy → just pq-wipe yes (re-seeds #announcements) → operator re-onboards web+native from seed, verifies cross-client DM. No just security-review recipe — review done via independent agent (captured in CLAUDE.md crypto section). All code shipped; this is the activation step.
how_to_resume: 2. **Read docs/PRIORITIES.md — top of TIER 0 is your next action.**
3. Run `git status` and `git log --oneline -10` to see recent state.
4. Read this file (orchestrator_state.json) — running journal of WHY past decisions were made.
5. Run `node scripts/agent-status.js` for per-scope coordinator-friendly summary.
6. Read docs/STATUS.md and docs/FEATURES.md for the static feature inventory.
7. Check data/coordination/agent_registry.ron for the canonical scope list.
8. THEN start work. End the session by updating PRIORITIES.md (what's next) and appending a decision to this file's `recent_decisions` (why we got here).
